mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above trend, real interest rates are restrictive. This suppresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.65% and CPIAUCSL at 301.808
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal: each 1% decline in PAYEMS predicts negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines do not correlate negatively with 5-day forward returns (Spearman rank correlation > -0.15 over any 3-year window), or the signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer demand momentum. When both series decline simultaneously month-over-month, it signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings forecasts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -1%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1450.0 thousand units and RSAFS at 691671.0 million dollars
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS do not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as higher production supports corporate profit margins and economic resilience.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% do not produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 58% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and forward returns remains below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 3.7% signals labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market anticipates future Federal Reserve rate cuts and lower inflation, reducing discount rates on equities.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with potential pivot to easing
failure_condition: UNRATE crossings above 3.7% do not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly interacts with the unemployment rate (UNRATE) to determine equity returns. In late-cycle expansions where GDPC1 growth slows below 2% annualized while UNRATE remains below 4%, the market reprices recession risk, producing negative 60-day returns as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2% AND UNRATE < 4%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 20182.491 billion chained 2017 dollars and UNRATE at 3.5%
failure_condition: The interaction signal does not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL month-over-month change below 0.2% signals disinflation and reduced real interest rate pressure. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as lower inflation expectations reduce equity risk premiums and support valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.2% do not produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 57% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary pressure or supply shock. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings under conditions of constrained supply and weakening demand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.3% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.0258 and UNRATE at 3.5%
failure_condition: The interaction signal does not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 64% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
